title: DNA Extraction with Heater Shaker - Part 2
author: Opentrons
description: This protocol performs a DNA extraction with the samples gathered from part 1 of the protocol. For specific protocol steps, please see below. A field for aspiration height when transferring the samples from the heater shaker to the magnetic module allows the user to ensure beads are not picked up, and and aspiration rate of 10% of the default is instilled to ensure so. Note: for reagent 4 in columns 4 & 5, reagent 5 in columns 6 & 7, and reagent 6 in columns 8 & 9, split the total calculated reagent volume EQUALLY between the pair of columns, as the protocol will circle between the pair of columns as the source. ---
steps_raw: This protocol performs a DNA extraction with the samples gathered from part 1 of the protocol. For specific protocol steps, please see below. A field for aspiration height when transferring the samples from the heater shaker to the magnetic module allows the user to ensure beads are not picked up, and and aspiration rate of 10% of the default is instilled to ensure so. Note: for reagent 4 in columns 4 & 5, reagent 5 in columns 6 & 7, and reagent 6 in columns 8 & 9, split the total calculated reagent volume EQUALLY between the pair of columns, as the protocol will circle between the pair of columns as the source. ---
Samples + controls transferred to magnetic module
Adding 240ul of reagent in column 4 of the reagent plate
Two washes with columns 5 and 6 of the reagent plate
30ul reagent added to samples and controls from column 7 of reagent plate
Samples + controls moved to fresh plate
